signal: seen.json structural bug: collection agent writes new URLs at JSON top level instead of inside 'entries' sub-dict.
action: Fixed seen.json data. Added structural constraint to feed-collect SKILL.md Step 5.
evidence_151: feed-collect v1.0.0 Step 5 shows correct entries wrapper format. v1.0.1 adds arXiv URL normalization. First split-architecture runs show correct seen.json writes. Need 2 more cycles to confirm.
evidence_152: 2026-03-18 18:00: seen.json has correct structure (description+entries keys, 0 top-level URL leaks, 75 entries). arXiv URLs all use abs/ format. Split architecture runs (feed-collect v1.0.1 + feed-score) maintain correct writes. 2 cycles of clean data.
evidence_153: 2026-03-18 21:00 CST: data/seen.json keys=['description','entries'], 0 top-level URL leaks, entries_len=75, arXiv entries all normalized to /abs/. No recurrence across 3 cycles.

signal: arXiv URL normalization rule from ct_031 fix (feed-collector v1.13.1) was not carried over to new feed-collect v1.0.0 during architectural split.
action: Added URL normalization rules (arXiv abs/pdf unification, utm removal, anchor stripping) to feed-collect v1.0.1 Step 3.
evidence_152: 2026-03-18 18:00: 5 arXiv entries in seen.json all use normalized abs/ format. feed-collect v1.0.1 Step 3 has explicit URL normalization rules. No duplicate arXiv articles in recent runs.
evidence_153: 2026-03-18 21:00 CST: seen.json still has 5 arXiv entries, all normalized to /abs/; no non-abs URLs detected.

signal: PCEC cron (95c267ae) timed out at 600s during 15:00 CST run. Daily logs growing very large due to feed pipeline split documentation. Opus model + massive context = timeout risk.
action: Updated PCEC cron Phase 1a to size-aware daily-log reading (head + markers + tail) to reduce context and timeout risk.
evidence_153: 2026-03-18 21:00 CST: cron 95c267ae payload updated with size-aware daily log reader loop (wc -l gating + rg markers + tail).
evidence_154: 2026-03-19 00:00 CST: This cycle running with size-aware reading. Monitoring completion time.

signal: feed-score occasionally produced incomplete scoreBreakdown (omitted `减分:0`) and used `git add -A`, risking accidental commits of unrelated changes.
action: Released feed-score v1.1.1: added repo-dirty guardrail, added Step 5 scoreBreakdown completeness check, replaced `git add -A` with staging only blog-day paths + allowlist check.

signal: Heartbeat misdiagnosed human-triggered SIGUSR1 restarts as 'periodic crash pattern'. Human had to intervene twice. Root cause: (1) heartbeat checked gwup but not logs for restart reason, (2) GLM-5-turbo format inertia in 120K token session ignored new HEARTBEAT.md rules.
action: Massive heartbeat restructure: moved logic to HEARTBEAT.md, added log forensics command, migrated to cron isolated session with lightContext:true to eliminate behavior inertia from long session history. Applied same lightContext fix to thread-archiver cron which had identical timeout pattern.

signal: GLM-5-turbo cron jobs timeout when full workspace context is injected. Pattern: heartbeat (298s timeout), thread-archiver (300s timeout). Both fixed by adding lightContext:true. Feed-broadcast (Sonnet, 300s) also timed out — increased to 600s.
action: Applied lightContext:true to thread-archiver cron. Increased feed-broadcast timeout to 600s. Pattern: always use lightContext for cron jobs that only need their own skill/prompt, not workspace files.
